steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you say in the morning to start a good day?
What do you say when someone says 'Hello' to you?
questions.hint: It's a friendly way to respond.

questions.question_text: What do you say when the day ends?
questions.hint: You say it before you sleep.

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👋

steps.title: First Day Greeting
steps.text: He says, 'Hello!' She says, 'Hi!' They listen and answer the teacher.

steps.audio_text: He says, 'Hi!' She listens carefully and then they both answer together.

questions.question_text: Who is ___? (Grammar question)
What do you say when you meet someone? (Vocabulary question)
She listens to the teacher. What is she doing? (Reading comprehension question)
True or False: You can say 'hi' instead of 'hello'.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Who said 'Hello' first in the video?
questions.hint: Think about the first person you see.

questions.question_text: What color is Kim's shirt?
questions.hint: Look at Kim when she talks.

questions.question_text: Where did the new friends meet?
questions.hint: They met at a place for learning.

items.definition: Used to refer to two or more people
Not old or not known before
Having existed for a long time

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👥

steps.title: Ben and Kim
steps.text: Today, Ben and Kim join our class. They are new. Ben is happy. Kim is shy.

steps.explanation: Use he or she for one person; they for two.

items.correctSentence: They are called Ben and Kim

steps.audio_text: Ben is new. He is called Ben. Kim and Ben are friends.

questions.question_text: ___ is new. (Grammar question)
Which word means 'not young'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'They are called friends.'
Who is called Ben? (Reading comprehension question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What sound does the letter 'A' make?
questions.hint: It's the start of the word 'apple'.

questions.question_text: Which letter comes after 'A'?
questions.hint: It's the beginning of the word 'ball'.

questions.question_text: What is the first letter in 'Cat'?

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 🔤

steps.title: The Spelling Game
steps.text: In class, they play a spelling game. She spells 'dog'. He spells 'cat'. They clap for the winner!

steps.rule_pattern: ___ is playing the game. Who is ___?

steps.audio_text: She spells the word 'alphabet'. He spells the letter 'A'. Who is spelling now?

steps.prompt: Practice speaking using the sentences below.

prompts.prompt: Say: 'They are playing the game. Who is playing?'
prompts.expected_text: They are playing the game. Who is playing?

questions.question_text: ___ is playing the game. Who is ___? (Grammar question)
Which word means 'a group of letters in order'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: A 'word' can have letters.
What does 'She spells a word.' mean? (Reading comprehension)
questions.correct_answer: She is saying the letters of a word.
options: She is saying the letters of a word.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet someone new?
What word means the opposite of new?
questions.hint: Think of something that has been around a long time.

questions.question_text: What should you do when you hear your name?
questions.hint: Use your ears to do this.

steps.title: New Friends
steps.text: They are new. Hello! My name is Tim. Who is she? She is Ana. Let's listen and answer.

steps.audio_text: Hi there! I'm Emma, and this is my friend Max. Let's say the alphabet and spell our names together. Are you ready?

questions.question: What are they going to do?

steps.prompt: Use the words you know to answer each prompt.

prompts.prompt: Say: 'I can spell my name: [Spell your name].'
prompts.expected_text: I can spell my name: E-M-M-A.

prompts.prompt: Say: 'Hello, my name is [Name] and I like to [activity].'
prompts.expected_text: Hello, my name is Emma and I like to play.

questions.question_text: I ___ Ben. (Grammar question)
Which emoji shows 'teacher'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'Hello' is a greeting.
Who is the new student? (Reading comprehension from lesson)
Fill in the blank: She ___ going to school.
